channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Navy veteran student recounts saving his ASU classmate from stabbing attack: 'Compelled to do something'
item.description: An Arizona State University student sprang into action to help classmate during "random" stabbing attack on the campus, saying that he felt "compelled" to help.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Latest: Harris campaigns in Wisconsin and Trump in Michigan in battle for 'blue wall' states
item.description: Democratic presidential candidate Kamala Harris is opening a two-day stay in Wisconsin and Michigan, and Republican candidate Donald Trump will be in Michigan on Thursday as the two candidates grapple for wins in the “blue wall” battleground states, which also include Pennsylvania.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Subway rider shot in the head by police files claim accusing officers of recklessly opening fire
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A lawyer for a New York City subway rider who was inadvertently shot in the head by police at a station has filed a legal claim against the city, accusing the officers of showing “carelessness and reckless” disregard for the lives of others when they opened fire on a man holding
item.dc:creator: CEDAR ATTANASIO, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Source: Dockworkers’ union to suspend strike until Jan. 15 to allow time to negotiate new contract
item.description: The union representing 45,000 striking U.S. dockworkers at East and Gulf coast ports has reached a deal to suspend a three-day strike until Jan. 15 to provide time to negotiate a new contract.
item.dc:creator: Tom Krisher, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: 3 officers convicted in Tyre Nichols fatal beating, 2 acquitted of civil rights charges
item.description: Three former Memphis officers were convicted Thursday of charges of witness tampering in the 2023 fatal beating of Tyre Nichols, and two were acquitted of federal civil rights violations in a death that sparked national protests and calls for broad changes in policing.
item.dc:creator: Megan Fayard, KRON4

item.title: Los Angeles prosecutors to review new evidence in Menendez brothers' 1996 murder conviction
item.description: Prosecutors in Los Angeles say they are reviewing new evidence in the case of the Menendez brothers to determine whether they should be serving life sentences for killing their parents in their Beverly Hills mansion more than 35 years ago.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Los Angeles D.A. to ‘reevaluate’ Menendez brothers’ convictions
item.description: Los Angeles County District Attorney George Gascón provided an update Thursday on the convictions of Erik and Lyle Menendez, who were convicted of killing their parents in August 1989.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: DC man cashes in $500K lottery ticket, finds another winning ticket for same payout a month later
item.description: A man from Washington, D.C., is another $500,000 richer after cashing in a second winning lottery ticket for a DC Lottery drawing in just as many months.
item.dc:creator: Celeste Houmard, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Melania Trump says she supports abortion rights, putting her at odds with the GOP
item.description: Melania Trump has revealed her support for abortion rights ahead of the release of her upcoming memoir. Her stated position exposes a stark contrast with her husband, Republican presidential nominee Donald Trump, on the crucial election issue.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINE FERNANDO, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Starlink internet service being sent to places hit by Helene
item.description: FEMA said 60 Starlink units have been sent to multiple places devastated by the hurricane, with more to come.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: Biden student loan forgiveness plan gets win in Georgia court
item.description: President Joe Biden’s student debt relief plan was dealt a win by a Georgia judge this week by removing one Republican challenger and letting the program advance for now after a temporarily restraining order expires.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Florida law banning homeless people from sleeping in public spaces takes effect
item.description: Under the law, municipalities are required to create designated spaces for camping and sleeping if homeless shelters are full.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KRON4

item.title: Frankie Valli addresses fan concerns about elder abuse
item.description: Valli still has a full slate of upcoming shows that will see him crisscrossing the nation, including five in Southern California.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, NewsNation

item.title: 10,000 fentanyl pills seized at Nashville bus station
item.description: Officers found a large bundle wrapped in duct tape with 10,000 pills that contained fentanyl, according to police.
item.dc:creator: Brittney Baird, NewsNation

item.title: Wawa named No. 1 convenience store in consumer satisfaction survey
item.description: Analysts noted that many of the top-ranked chains specialized in ready-to-eat foods prepared in the stores' kitchens.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Yakubovsky, KRON4

item.title: Men accused of killing Jocelyn Nungaray believed to be Venezuelan gang members: search warrant
item.description: Search warrants reveal how investigators have looked into the social media accounts of the men accused of murdering 12-year-old Jocelyn Nungaray.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Melania Trump: ‘We are dealing with much more danger’
item.description: The former first lady said Wednesday that she thinks Americans need to listen to one another, even if they hold different beliefs.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: 3 officers convicted in Tyre Nichols fatal beating but 2 are acquitted of civil rights charges
item.description: Three former Memphis police officers have been convicted in the 2023 fatal beating of Tyre Nichols, but were acquitted of the harshest charges they faced.
item.dc:creator: Adrian Sainz, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: How you can help after Helene
item.description: Reporters who rode out the storm will explain what Americans faced in the hours after Helene arrived. Red Cross representatives will also explain how your donations are being used to help Americans across 10 states get back on their feet.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, ABC 4

item.title: Meet Spot, the LAPD's new crimefighting robot dog
item.description: The futuristic four-legged machine, which boasts the size and agility of a 70-pound golden retriever, has been met with both fascination and concern.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Irate residents halt construction of migrant shelter
item.description: Residents of a southeast Juarez neighborhood are demanding the government call off plans to build a migrant shelter near their children’s schools.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, NewsNation

item.title: Florida communities hit three times by hurricanes grapple with how and whether to rebuild
item.description: HORSESHOE BEACH, Fla. (AP) — It was just a month ago that Brooke Hiers left the state-issued emergency trailer where her family had lived since Hurricane Idalia slammed into her Gulf Coast fishing village of Horseshoe Beach in August 2023.
item.dc:creator: KATE PAYNE and DAVID R. MARTIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Grandparents killed by fallen tree during Helene are found hugging
item.description: A South Carolina couple were found hugging each other after a tree fell on their bedroom and killed them.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Hurricane Helene scrambles politics in 3 battleground states
item.description: The 500-mile-long path of destruction cut by Hurricane Helene has scrambled the politics of three battleground states that could determine control of the White House and Senate: North Carolina, Georgia and Florida.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, WFLA, NBC

item.title: The fate of Nibi the beaver lands in court as rescuers try to stop her release into the wild
item.description: Whether a 2-year-old beaver named Nibi gets to stay with the rescuers she has known since she was a baby or must be released into the wild as winter approaches in Massachusetts has ended up in court — and caused such an uproar that even the governor has weighed in.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Judge rejects computer repairman's defamation claims over reports on Hunter Biden laptop
item.description: DOVER, Del. (AP) — A computer repairman at the center of a controversy over Hunter Biden's laptop has lost his defamation case against news outlets, the president's son and Joe Biden's presidential campaign.
item.dc:creator: RANDALL CHASE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump takes lead over Harris in North Carolina Quinnipiac survey
item.description: Trump leads with 49 percent support, Harris earns 47 percent support, Green Party candidate Jill Stein gets 1 percent support and Justice For All Party candidate Cornel West gets 1 percent support in the Quinnipiac University poll conducted in late September and released Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: McDonald’s is bringing back its famous ‘Boo Buckets,’ with a new color
item.description: McDonald's famous Boo Buckets are making a return just in time for Halloween, and a new color has joined the lineup this year.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Why Tuesday's vice presidential debate could matter more than history suggests
item.description: Tim Walz and JD Vance might have the chance to reshape the political landscape Tuesday in their first and only debate.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Ken Page, who starred in 'Cats' on Broadway and voiced Oogie Boogie, dies at 70
item.description: Ken Page, a stage and screen actor who starred alongside Beyoncé in “Dreamgirls,” introduced Broadway audiences to Old Deuteronomy in “Cats” and scared generations of kids as the voice of Oogie Boogie, the villain of the 1993 animated holiday film ”The Nightmare Before Christmas,″ has died.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Iran fires at least 180 missiles into Israel as regionwide conflict grows
item.description: Iran launched at least 180 missiles into Israel on Tuesday, the latest in a series of escalating attacks in a yearslong conflict between Israel and Iran and its Arab allies that threatens to push the Middle East closer toward a regionwide war.
item.dc:creator: Aamer Madhani, ABC 4

item.title: Crews search for survivors in North Carolina's mountains days after Helene's deluge
item.description: SWANNANOA, N.C. (AP) — Rescuers fanned out across the mountains of western North Carolina on Tuesday in search of anyone still unaccounted for since Hurricane Helene’s remnants caused catastrophic damage to the Southeast, with a death toll nearing 140 people.
item.dc:creator: ERIK VERDUZCO and JEFFREY COLLINS Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Helene and other storms dumped a whopping 40 trillion gallons of rain on the South
item.description: More than 40 trillion gallons of rain drenched the Southeast United States in the last week from Hurricane Helene and a run-of-the-mill rainstorm that sloshed in ahead of it — an unheard of amount of water that has stunned experts.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Favre tries to expand his defamation lawsuit against Mississippi auditor over welfare spending
item.description: Favre is not facing criminal charges, but he is among more than three dozen people or businesses the state is suing to try to recover misspent money through the Temporary Assistance for Needy Families program.
item.dc:creator: EMILY WAGSTER PETTUS, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: 3 ex-officers did not testify at their trial in Tyre Nichols' death
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. (AP) — Lawyers for three former Memphis officers rested their cases Monday without calling them to testify in their own defense against charges that they violated Tyre Nichols’ civil rights in a beating that proved fatal following a 2023 traffic stop.
item.dc:creator: ADRIAN SAINZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Man is sentenced to 35 years for shooting 2 Jewish men as they left Los Angeles synagogues
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — A California man was sentenced Monday to 35 years in prison for shooting and wounding two Jewish men as they left synagogues in Los Angeles last year, federal prosecutors said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Gavin Creel, Tony Award-winning veteran of Broadway musicals, dies at 48
item.description: Creel's publicist said he died at his home in Manhattan of metastatic melanotic peripheral nerve sheath sarcoma.
item.dc:creator: Mark Kennedy, Fox 5
